Container.Name: SelfDashboard
Container.Overview: SelfDashboard — modular home dashboard (Unraid, Emby, Docker widget, etc.). Shared config: with the Config Storage volume on /app/data, the app saves dashboard.json on the server (dashboards, widgets, settings) so every browser stays in sync; the browser still uses localStorage as a fast cache. Backup that host folder. Optional env SELFDASHBOARD_DATA_DIR overrides the in-container data directory (image default: /app/data). Docker widget: bind-mount the host Docker socket (see Docker Socket). Extra parameter --group-add=281 matches the usual Unraid docker group GID for socket access (change if stat -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock differs). Builtin plugins are compiled into the image from src/lib/pluginLoader.ts (not bind-mounted). The optional Custom Plugins Path maps to /app/plugins/custom; the stock image does not auto-load TS plugins from there — use it only if you need the folder for your own files or a custom-built image. Custom plugin workflow: see README / docs/PLUGIN_DEV.md and rebuild the image.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the SelfDashboard web interface.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Your local timezone. Example: Europe/Berlin, America/New_York

Config.Name: Config Storage
Config.Description: Host folder mounted at /app/data. The app writes dashboard.json here (dashboards, widgets, global settings) so all browsers share the same layout; localStorage is still used as a cache. Keep this on persistent storage and include it in backups. Optional container env SELFDASHBOARD_DATA_DIR overrides the path inside the container (default in image: /app/data).

Config.Name: Custom Plugins Path
Config.Description: Optional host folder mounted at /app/plugins/custom. Builtin plugins come from the image (see src/lib/pluginLoader.ts in the repo) — they are not loaded by editing this path alone. The stock image does not auto-register arbitrary TypeScript plugins from this directory at runtime; remove the mapping if unused, or use it for your own files / a custom image you build.

Config.Name: CrowdSec Data
Config.Description: Optional. Mount CrowdSec appdata (contains crowdsec.db) read-only at /crowdsec-data for the CrowdSec plugin. Unraid default: /mnt/user/appdata/crowdsec/data. In plugin settings use path /crowdsec-data/crowdsec.db. Clear if you do not use CrowdSec.

Config.Name: CrowdSec Postoverflows
Config.Description: Optional. For CrowdSec plugin dynamic whitelist: mount postoverflows read-write at /crowdsec-postoverflows. Default whitelist path: /crowdsec-postoverflows/s01-whitelist/my-whitelist.yaml. Also requires Docker socket. Clear if unused.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Same as -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock (read-only). Required for the Docker widget. If you see EACCES, the template adds --group-add=281 (typical Unraid docker GID); adjust Extra Parameters if your `stat -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock` differs. Only this Unraid's containers, not remote Docker.

Config.Name: Node Environment
Config.Description: Leave production unless you know you need development mode.
